data.title: Bitcoin holds steady at $67,000 amidst volatility; $188.97 million liquidations in 24hrs. ETF flows: BTC -$104.9M, ETH +$48.6M. Regulatory optimism on CLARITY Act. News source: Benzinga.com.
data.description: Bitcoin is hovering around $67,000 despite recent volatility and significant ETF withdrawals, with liquidations reaching $188.97 million in the last day. Investors are hopeful about potential advancements with the CLARITY Act for clearer crypto regulations in the U.S. Bitcoin ETFs experienced $104.9 million in outflows on Tuesday, while Ethereum ETFs saw $48.6 million in inflows. Prices for top cryptocurrencies are: Bitcoin at $67,332.41, Ethereum at $1,982.82, Solana at $83.54, XRP at $1.47, Dogecoin at $0.1002, and Shiba Inu at $0.56446. The meme coin sector is relatively stable, declining by 0.9% in the last 24 hours. More details can be found in the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: WLFI surges over 10% before Mar-a-Lago event. World Liberty Financial gains traction with $500M UAE deal controversy.
data.description: The DeFi project World Liberty Financial, supported by Trump, has experienced an increase in value due to significant purchases by large investors and a short squeeze. This comes as the project faces scrutiny over a deal worth $500 million with the UAE.

data.title: FDA grants Breakthrough Device status to NG Biotech and Hardy Diagnostics for 2 rapid tests targeting drug-resistant pathogens, aiding in the fight against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR).
data.description: On February 18, 2026, NG Biotech and Hardy Diagnostics revealed a significant advancement in combating Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) by receiving FDA approval for their Breakthrough Device. This partnership, based in Santa Maria, Calif., and Guipry, France, marks a crucial development in the fight against AMR. The collaboration aims to address the growing concern of drug resistance.

data.title: SolarEdge Technologies beat Q4 earnings estimates by 64.1%, with EPS at $-0.14 vs $-0.39, revenue up $139.14 million.
data.description: SolarEdge Technologies (NASDAQ: SEDG) released its Q4 earnings results on Wednesday, February 18, 2026, at 06:55 AM. The company surpassed estimated earnings by 64.1%, posting an EPS of $-0.14 compared to the expected $-0.39. Revenue saw a substantial increase of $139.14 million from the previous year. For further details on SolarEdge Technologies' earnings performance and more, the complete story can be found on a finance news website.

data.title: Sharon AI lists on Nasdaq with $125M IPO, offering 4,166,666 shares at $30 each.
data.description: SharonAI Holdings Inc. and its subsidiaries have revealed the pricing of their initial public offering in the United States. The offering consists of 4,166,666 shares of its Class A Ordinary Common Stock at a public price of $30.00 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of around $125 million. This amount is calculated before accounting for underwriting discounts, commissions, and other offering expenses.

data.title: Starlab Space and Helogen collaborate to enhance life sciences research in microgravity, boosting scalability and regulatory-grade manufacturing, advancing discoveries.
data.description: Houston-based Starlab Space LLC has revealed a collaboration with Helogen Corporation to enhance low Earth orbit research. This partnership aims to revolutionize life sciences studies in microgravity by transitioning from individual experiments to larger, continuous discoveries. By teaming up with Helogen Corporation, a specialist in automated industrial processes for space, Starlab Space LLC looks to enable scalable manufacturing and autonomous biological operations in orbit. This strategic alliance signifies a significant step forward in advancing access to space research opportunities.

data.title: PROG Holdings, Inc. reports Q4 2025 results, showing strength & resilience. CEO Steve Michaels happy with disciplined execution.
data.description: In Salt Lake City, PROG Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:PRG) revealed financial results for the fourth quarter ending on December 31, 2025. The fintech holding company overseeing Progressive Leasing, Purchasing Power, Four Technologies, and MoneyApp reported a successful Q4 and full-year 2025. CEO Steve Michaels highlighted the company's disciplined execution and the robustness of PROG's multi-product platform amid a tough retail landscape and external factors. The quarter showcased the company's resilience, portraying a positive outlook for PROG Holdings.

data.title: Delta Galil Industries, Ltd. reported financial results for Q4 & FY 2025. Key numbers: December 31, 2025.
data.description: An Israel-based company, Delta Galil Industries, Ltd., disclosed its financial performance for the 4th quarter and full year that concluded on December 31, 2025.

data.title: North Carolina AG Jeff Jackson secures ruling protecting homebuyers from predatory contracts, supported by ALTA, NCLTA, and AARP.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, the American Land Title Association (ALTA), alongside the North Carolina Land Title Association (NCLTA) and AARP, praised North Carolina Attorney General Jeff Jackson for successfully obtaining a legal victory. This comes as a significant development as these organizations represent the land title insurance sector. The collaboration between ALTA and NCLTA emphasizes the importance of this achievement, showcasing the unified support from various industry bodies. Such initiatives demonstrate the commitment to upholding legal standards and ensuring consumer protection in North Carolina. This event reflects a broader effort to maintain integrity within the state’s legal and insurance systems, promoting transparency and accountability. The involvement of AARP in this acknowledgment further underscores the impact of this legal triumph, signaling a positive outcome for the broader community and stakeholders.

data.title: Tactile Systems Tech beats Q4 earnings, sales up 21% to $103.59M. Forecasts 2026 sales $357M-$365M. Adjusted EBITDA $49M-$51M. - Benzinga
data.description: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: TCMD) reported strong fourth-quarter earnings, surpassing expectations with earnings of 46 cents per share compared to the consensus of 44 cents. The company also revealed the acquisition of LymphaTech. Sales for the quarter saw a significant increase of 21% to $103.59 million, outperforming Wall Street estimates of $93.80 million, driven by growth in lymphedema and airway clearance product lines. Looking ahead, Tactile Systems projects sales for fiscal year 2026 to be in the range of $357 million to $365 million, higher than the consensus of $347.94 million. Additionally, the company anticipates adjusted EBITDA to be between $49 million and $51 million. Tactile Systems highlighted their successful execution of core growth strategies in 2025, achieving double-digit revenue growth, expanding gross margin and adjusted EBITDA, and generating robust cash flow.

data.title: Social engineering influenced 65% of crypto cases examined by AMLBot in 2025, with scams targeting investors through impersonation.
data.description: According to recent investigations conducted by AMLBot, the primary method used in targeting crypto investors last year was through social engineering and impersonation-related scams. These scams accounted for the highest frequency among all attack vectors.

data.title: PROG Holdings Q4 earnings beat estimates by 48.33% with EPS of $0.89. Revenue down $48.73M from last year.
data.description: PROG Holdings (NYSE: PRG) released its Q4 earnings on February 18, 2026, at 07:30 AM. The notable highlights include surpassing estimated earnings by 48.33% with an EPS of $0.89 in comparison to the estimated $0.6. Revenue experienced a decline of $48.73 million from the corresponding period the previous year. For a comprehensive analysis of the company's earnings history, investors can explore further details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: BrightView (NYSE: BV) expands golf partnership with The Villages®, managing over 50% of its 400 golf holes.
data.description: BrightView Landscapes, a top commercial landscaping services provider and golf course maintenance company listed on the NYSE under BV, has revealed an expansion in its partnership with The Villages® in Florida. The company now manages over 50% of the Community Development District's 400 golf holes, representing a substantial increase from the previous approximately 20%. This means BrightView now oversees more than 20 nine-hole golf courses within The Villages.

data.title: Medline to participate in Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference in Miami Beach on March 11, 2026 at 9:30am ET.
data.description: In Northfield, Illinois, on February 18, 2026, Medline Inc. revealed that its CEO, Jim Boyle, along with CFO Mike Drazin, will be speaking at the Barclays 28th Global Healthcare Conference in Miami Beach, Florida. The presentation is set for Wednesday, March 11, 2026, at 9:30am Eastern Time. Medline is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol MDLN.

data.title: Creative Realities repurchases Slipstream warrants, reducing dilution exposure and improving visibility for shareholders.
data.description: Decreases dilution exposure in the interest of shareholders by improving visibility. This move aims to safeguard shareholder value and enhance transparency in the company's operations.
